title: Pixel Quest Builder
pitch: Build retro-style RPG browser games with zero code — drag tiles, define quests, export in minutes.
audience: Game hobbyists and educators who want to make browser games without learning a game engine
problem: Building a playable browser game from scratch takes weeks; Pixel Quest Builder gets you from idea to shareable link in an afternoon.
demoScript: Open the editor and select a tileset from the built-in library
Drag tiles onto the map canvas to build your world
Click a character to define its quest dialogue and win conditions
Hit Export and share the generated URL — fully playable in any browser

title: FocusFlow Timer
pitch: An adaptive Pomodoro timer that lengthens your focus blocks the longer your streak — stay in flow.
audience: Knowledge workers and students who struggle to maintain focus during long work sessions
problem: Fixed Pomodoro intervals ignore your actual focus state, breaking momentum right when you hit a flow state.
demoScript: Start a focus session — the timer begins at 25 minutes by default
Complete the session without breaking; your next block automatically extends to 30 minutes
Check your daily streak graph to see your focus patterns over time
Set a daily session target and watch the app nudge you back when you drift

title: Palette Drop
pitch: Generate, tweak, and export full color palettes from a single seed color — Figma export included.
audience: Designers and developers who need a consistent color palette fast without opening a complex design tool
problem: Picking a harmonious color palette from scratch wastes hours; Palette Drop generates a complete, accessible scale in seconds.
demoScript: Enter any hex color as your seed
Palette Drop generates a 10-shade scale, complementary pairs, and contrast scores
Adjust tones with the lightness and saturation sliders
Export as CSS variables, Tailwind config, or Figma tokens with one click

title: Budget Blip
pitch: Privacy-first personal finance tracker — CSV import, category tagging, monthly summaries, no account needed.
audience: Privacy-conscious individuals who want to track spending without handing their data to a fintech app
problem: Every budgeting app wants your bank login or charges a subscription; Budget Blip runs entirely in your browser with no server.
demoScript: Export a CSV from your bank and drag it into Budget Blip
Transactions are parsed and displayed instantly — no upload, everything stays local
Click any transaction to assign it a category tag
Open the Summary view to see a monthly breakdown by category

title: Draft Spark
pitch: Distraction-free writing with smart un-stuck prompts — no AI, just contextual nudges when you stop typing.
audience: Writers, bloggers, and students who experience writer's block and want a focused drafting environment
problem: AI writing tools take over your voice; Draft Spark helps you get unstuck with prompts, then gets out of the way.
demoScript: Open a new draft and start typing in the clean, minimal editor
Stop for 10 seconds — a gentle prompt appears from the local library (never AI-generated)
Accept, dismiss, or swap the prompt with one click
Export your finished draft as Markdown or plain text

title: Locale Lens
pitch: Discover and bookmark local businesses on a privacy-first map — no trackers, no accounts, no ads.
audience: Local community members who want to find and share neighborhood spots without using Google Maps or Yelp
problem: Every local discovery app profits from your location data; Locale Lens uses OpenStreetMap and stores everything in localStorage.
demoScript: Open the map — it loads your area using OpenStreetMap tiles, no account required
Search for a business type and click any pin to see basic info
Bookmark spots with a single click; they persist in your browser's localStorage
Export your bookmarks as JSON to back them up or share with a friend
